\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1079}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}}}\moddef{attract loop~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $0569
\nwlinkedidentc{ATTRACT_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{ATTRACT_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}

    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{CONTEXT_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{ATTRACT_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{ATTRACT{\_}LOOP}}{ATTRACT:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{ATTRACT{\_}LOOP}}{ATTRACT:unLOOP}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}}\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1080}\nwdocspar

\chapter{Resident Routines}

\label{sec:resident_interp}

EALDR copies 768 bytes from {\Tt{}{\$}A300\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}A5FF\nwendquote} to {\Tt{}{\$}0500\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}07FF\nwendquote}
before entering the main program.  This region contains a second bytecode
interpreter, separate from the EALDR VM, that runs during gameplay and
the attract screen.  It uses the same XOR encryption scheme as the EALDR
VM (low byte XOR {\Tt{}{\$}03\nwendquote}, high byte XOR {\Tt{}{\$}D9\nwendquote}).

\paragraph{Disk origin.}
The source data at {\Tt{}{\$}A300\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}A5FF\nwendquote} is part of the EALDR image that
BOOT1 loads from tracks~1--2 into {\Tt{}{\$}A000\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}BFFF\nwendquote}.  Within
track~1, the three pages map to physical sectors via the custom loader
interleave:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{cccc}
\toprule
\thead{Page} & \thead{Physical sector} & \thead{DOS sector} & \thead{File offset} \\
\midrule
\$A3 & 6  & 12 & \$1C00 \\
\$A4 & 8  & 11 & \$1B00 \\
\$A5 & 10 & 10 & \$1A00 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

After {\Tt{}GAME{\_}INIT\nwendquote}, the relocation
{\Tt{}{\$}2000--{\$}32FF\nwendquote}~$\to$~{\Tt{}{\$}9600--{\$}A8FF\nwendquote} overwrites the original
{\Tt{}{\$}A300\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}A5FF\nwendquote} with HRCG/font data.  From that point on, only the
copy at {\Tt{}{\$}0500\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}07FF\nwendquote} survives.  The script pointer table
(\S\ref{sec:script_engine}) references the original {\Tt{}{\$}A300\nwendquote}-based
addresses, which is safe because scripts~3--7 run only during attract
mode, before {\Tt{}GAME{\_}INIT\nwendquote}.

The reset vector points here.  Simply jumps to {\Tt{}ATTRACT{\_}LOOP\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1081}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-W2PWE4EL5SM76XCHIGY3YJKIPY000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-W2PWE4EL5SM76XCHIGY3YJKIPY000000-1}}}\moddef{stub entry~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-W2PWE4EL5SM76XCHIGY3YJKIPY000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $0500
\nwlinkedidentc{STUB_ENTRY}{PYNWmai0-W2PWE4EL5SM76XCHIGY3YJKIPY000000-1}:
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{ATTRACT_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-Y3PF34ZZHWG7GMSG7XZGBPWN5A000000-1}
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{STUB{\_}ENTRY}}{STUB:unENTRY}{PYNWmai0-W2PWE4EL5SM76XCHIGY3YJKIPY000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{STUB{\_}ENTRY}}{STUB:unENTRY}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{ATTRACT{\_}LOOP}}{ATTRACT:unLOOP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{ATTRACT{\_}LOOP}}{ATTRACT:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-W2PWE4EL5SM76XCHIGY3YJKIPY000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1082}\nwdocspar
This 321-byte region between {\Tt{}ATTRACT{\_}LOOP\nwendquote} and {\Tt{}CONTEXT{\_}SWAP\nwendquote}
contains EALDR VM bytecodes interleaved with native 6502 routines.
The bytecodes drive the attract-screen display and the
copy-protection verifier that runs before the game starts.

Embedded within the bytecodes are several native routines called via
VM {\Tt{}CALL0\nwendquote} opcodes:
\begin{itemize}
\item \textbf{SEARCH\_ADDR\_FIELD} (\$05FD--\$0649): searches for a
      disk address field (D5~AA~96 prologue) and decodes 4\&4 nibble
      data.  Self-modifying code at \$064C stores the retry count.
\item \textbf{DELAY\_LOOP} (\$0681--\$068B): nested countdown delay
      for motor spin-up timing.
\item \textbf{READ\_AND\_VERIFY} (\$064B--\$0667): calls
      {\Tt{}SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD\nwendquote}, then reads and checksums a data area
      while toggling drive phases for half-track seeks.
\end{itemize}

Three short native utility routines at the end (\$06A6--\$06B2)
provide VM-to-native exit points and bytecode decryption:
\begin{itemize}
\item \$06A6: swap context and return carry clear (OK).
\item \$06AB: swap context and return carry set (error).
\item \$06B0: decrypt high byte (EOR~\#\$D9).
\end{itemize}

The address at \$0678 doubles as RWTS scratch storage during disk
operations.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1083}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}}}\moddef{resident data and bytecodes~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $0572
    SUBROUTINE resident_data_and_bytecodes
; EALDR VM bytecodes + embedded native disk I/O routines ($0572-$06B2)
; Bytecode encryption: low XOR $03, high XOR $D9, immediate XOR $4C
;
; Native routines within this region:
;   $05FD  \nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_ADDR_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1} -- scan for D5 AA 96 address prologue
;   $064B  \nwlinkedidentc{READ_AND_VERIFY}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1} -- read data with phase toggling
;   $0681  \nwlinkedidentc{DELAY_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1} -- nested countdown for motor timing
;
; $0572-$06A5: VM bytecodes (output as raw data)
    HEX 0463dc0774028fdf0462dc072c028fdf ; $0572
    HEX 0464dc0774028fdf05a4dc0290dc00a7 ; $0582
    HEX dc05a4dc029fdc00a7dc074e028fdf00 ; $0592
    HEX 71dc08a5df04ea1903b301ab2503b301 ; $05A2
    HEX ab2504ed19034c06e1de05ccdc05ccdc ; $05B2
    HEX 05ccdc05ccdc04eb1904e0de09034f06 ; $05C2
    HEX 4fdf014edf04e419014edf014edf04e2 ; $05D2
    HEX 190569df034f064fdf014edf04e41901 ; $05E2
    HEX 4edf014edf04e6190569df09a0ffae4c ; $05F2
    HEX 06adecc010fbc9d5f00788d0f4cad0f1 ; $0602
    HEX 60adecc010fbc9aaf00588d0e43860ad ; $0612
    HEX ecc010fbc996f00588d0d63860a002ad ; $0622
    HEX ecc010fb2a8550adecc010fb25508550 ; $0632
    HEX 888e4c0610e918a201600320fe05b003 ; $0642
    HEX 200305a9006de2078de207ade0c0ade2 ; $0652
    HEX c0ade4c0ade6c060033c0182df04e319 ; $0662
    HEX 04e11904e71904e51903640182df09a2 ; $0672
    HEX 20cad0fd38e901d0f6600603dd0a8ddf ; $0682
    HEX 028fdf048cdf01b3df07b301b3df068c ; $0692
    HEX df008fdf                         ; $06A2
;
; $06A6-$06B2: Native utility routines
\nwlinkedidentc{VM_EXIT_OK}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{CONTEXT_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}                 ; swap back to main ZP
    CLC                                  ; carry clear = success
    RTS
\nwlinkedidentc{VM_EXIT_ERR}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{CONTEXT_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}                 ; swap back to main ZP
    SEC                                  ; carry set = error
    RTS
\nwlinkedidentc{DECRYPT_HIGH_BYTE}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}:
    EOR     #$D9                         ; decrypt high byte of address
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DECRYPT{\_}HIGH{\_}BYTE}}{DECRYPT:unHIGH:unBYTE}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{VM:unEXIT:unERR}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK}}{VM:unEXIT:unOK}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DECRYPT{\_}HIGH{\_}BYTE}}{DECRYPT:unHIGH:unBYTE}}\\{{\nwixident{VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{VM:unEXIT:unERR}}\\{{\nwixident{VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK}}{VM:unEXIT:unOK}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}}\\{{\nwixident{DELAY{\_}LOOP}}{DELAY:unLOOP}}\\{{\nwixident{READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY}}{READ:unAND:unVERIFY}}\\{{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DELAY{\_}LOOP}}{DELAY:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY}}{READ:unAND:unVERIFY}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-YRAQZG4RK7BUU66N6TZLZZQ3CE000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1084}\nwdocspar
Swaps 16 bytes between zero page {\Tt{}{\$}50\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}5F\nwendquote} and {\Tt{}{\$}07E5\nwendquote}--{\Tt{}{\$}07F4\nwendquote}.
This gives the resident interpreter its own private set of zero-page
temporaries that don't interfere with the main program.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1085}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}}}\moddef{context swap~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $06E0
\nwlinkedidentc{CONTEXT_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{CONTEXT_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}

    LDX     #$0F
.loop:
    LDA     $50,X  ; load ZP byte
    PHA            ; save on stack
    LDA     $7E5,X ; load shadow byte
    STA     $50,X  ; -> ZP
    PLA            ; recover old ZP byte
    STA     $7E5,X ; -> shadow
    DEX
    BPL     .loop
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1086}\nwdocspar
The 14 bytes between {\Tt{}CONTEXT{\_}SWAP\nwendquote} and {\Tt{}RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH\nwendquote}
({\Tt{}{\$}06F2--{\$}06FF\nwendquote}) are unreferenced and decode as the high-bit ASCII
fragment ``\texttt{DON'T BREAK TH}''\,---likely a truncated development
message.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1087}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-LVNCRQLPWM7GNKGQEJBTLBYX5E000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-LVNCRQLPWM7GNKGQEJBTLBYX5E000000-1}}}\moddef{context swap padding~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-LVNCRQLPWM7GNKGQEJBTLBYX5E000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $06F2
; Residual string: "DON'T BREAK TH" (high-bit ASCII, truncated)
    HEX     c4cfcea7d4a0c2d2c5c1cba0d4c8
\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1088}\nwdocspar
Dispatches one bytecode instruction using a stack trick.  X and Y hold
the bytecode pointer from the previous iteration.  They are pushed onto
the stack at entry, then {\Tt{}PULL{\_}TWO{\_}RET{\_}ADDRS\nwendquote} rearranges the stack
so the saved X/Y values end up in {\Tt{}{\$}52/{\$}53\nwendquote} as the new bytecode pointer.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1089}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}}}\moddef{resident dispatch~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $06B3
\nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}

    TXA                              ; \\
    PHA                              ;  | save X, Y on stack
    TYA                              ;  | (these ARE the bytecode pointer)
    PHA                              ; /
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{PULL_TWO_RET_ADDRS}{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}       ; rearrange stack, jump to .resume

    ; Control returns here via JMP ($54):
.resume:
    PLA                              ; \\  recover saved Y
    STA     $52                      ;  | $52/$53 = bytecode pointer
    PLA                              ;  | recover saved X
    STA     $53                      ; /

    LDY     #$04                     ; skip 4 bytes
RESIDENT_DISPATCH_FETCH:
.fetch:
    LDA     ($52),Y                  ; read opcode
    INY
    BNE     .no_carry
    INC     $53
.no_carry:
    TAX                              ; opcode -> X (also saves position)
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DISPATCH_TABLE}{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1},X
    STA     resident_jmp+1           ; self-modify: JMP low byte
resident_jmp:
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DECODE_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1} ; self-modified -> $07xx
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{PULL{\_}TWO{\_}RET{\_}ADDRS}}{PULL:unTWO:unRET:unADDRS}}\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDECODE:unDISPATCH}}\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH{\_}TABLE}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH:unTABLE}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{PULL{\_}TWO{\_}RET{\_}ADDRS}}{PULL:unTWO:unRET:unADDRS}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDECODE:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH{\_}TABLE}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH:unTABLE}{PYNWmai0-2FARJ2CLNXYFYA6O756BE3RHTA000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1090}\nwdocspar
The 18-byte table maps opcode indices to handler addresses in page {\Tt{}{\$}07\nwendquote}:

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1091}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1}}}\moddef{resident dispatch table~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $06D3
\nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DISPATCH_TABLE}{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1}:
    HEX     00 27 55 5F 6D 8A AD B9
    HEX     4F A2 CE 26 79
; last 5 bytes overlap with \nwlinkedidentc{CONTEXT_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-A5IY6LARV2ZVHGEJNTTVGGK3PU000000-1} code at $06E0
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH{\_}TABLE}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH:unTABLE}{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH{\_}TABLE}}{RESIDENT:unDISPATCH:unTABLE}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{CONTEXT{\_}SWAP}}{CONTEXT:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-LZD7UO27WADGX7M4TS77RNCD74000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1092}\nwdocspar
Opcode \$00 handler---the resident interpreter's GOTO instruction.
Decodes an address and redirects execution there.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1093}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}}}\moddef{resident decode dispatch~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $0700
\nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DECODE_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DECODE_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}

    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DECODE_ENCRYPTED_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}   ; -> $54/$55
    LDA     $54                     ; \\
    STA     $52                     ;  | $52/$53 = decoded address
    LDA     $55                     ;  |
    STA     $53                     ; /
    LDY     #$00
    JMP     RESIDENT_DISPATCH_FETCH ; fetch from new location
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDECODE:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDECODE:unDISPATCH}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DECODE{\_}ENCRYPTED{\_}ADDR}}{DECODE:unENCRYPTED:unADDR}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DECODE{\_}ENCRYPTED{\_}ADDR}}{DECODE:unENCRYPTED:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1094}\nwdocspar
\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1095}\nwdocspar

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{ccl}
\toprule
\thead{Opcode} & \thead{Table byte} & \thead{Handler address} \\
\midrule
\$00 & \$00 & \$0700 (RESIDENT\_DECODE\_DISPATCH) \\
\$01 & \$27 & \$0727 \\
\$02 & \$55 & \$0755 \\
\$03 & \$5F & \$075F \\
\$04 & \$6D & \$076D \\
\$05 & \$8A & \$078A \\
\$06 & \$AD & \$07AD \\
\$07 & \$B9 & \$07B9 \\
\$08 & \$4F & \$074F \\
\$09 & \$A2 & \$07A2 \\
\$0A & \$CE & \$07CE \\
\$0B & \$26 & \$0726 \\
\$0C & \$79 & \$0779 \\
\$0D & \$A2 & \$07A2 \\
\$0E & \$0F & \$070F \\
\$0F & \$B5 & \$07B5 \\
\$10 & \$50 & \$0750 \\
\$11 & \$48 & \$0748 \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Opcodes \$09 and \$0D share the same handler (\$07A2).

Decodes a 2-byte encrypted address from the bytecode stream.
Uses the same XOR scheme as the EALDR VM.  The decoded address is
left in {\Tt{}{\$}54/{\$}55\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1096}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}}}\moddef{decode encrypted addr~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $0710
\nwlinkedidentc{DECODE_ENCRYPTED_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DECODE_ENCRYPTED_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}

    LDA     ($52),Y ; read low byte
    EOR     #$03    ; decrypt
    INY
    BNE     .no_carry1
    INC     $53
.no_carry1:
    STA     $54     ; decoded low
    LDA     ($52),Y ; read high byte
    INY
    BNE     .no_carry2
    INC     $53
.no_carry2:
    EOR     #$D9    ; decrypt
    STA     $55     ; decoded high
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DECODE{\_}ENCRYPTED{\_}ADDR}}{DECODE:unENCRYPTED:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-QPGMZCBHONP3ALTVEWGVE7O2I4000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DECODE{\_}ENCRYPTED{\_}ADDR}}{DECODE:unENCRYPTED:unADDR}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1097}\nwdocspar
\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1098}\nwdocspar

{\Tt{}PULL{\_}TWO{\_}RET{\_}ADDRS\nwendquote} pulls both return addresses from the stack
(its own and the caller's), increments each by 1 (since RTS pushes
addr\textminus{}1), and jumps through {\Tt{}{\$}54/{\$}55\nwendquote} back to the code
after the {\Tt{}JSR\nwendquote} in {\Tt{}RESIDENT{\_}DISPATCH\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1099}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}}}\moddef{pull two ret addrs~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $0738
\nwlinkedidentc{PULL_TWO_RET_ADDRS}{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{PULL_TWO_RET_ADDRS}{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}

    PLA           ; own return addr (low)
    STA     $54
    PLA           ; own return addr (high)
    STA     $55
    PLA           ; caller's return addr (low)
    STA     $52
    PLA           ; caller's return addr (high)
    STA     $53
    INC     $52   ; +1 (RTS pushed addr-1)
    INC     $54   ; +1
    BNE     .done
    INC     $55   ; carry
.done:
    JMP     ($54) ; jump back to .resume
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{PULL{\_}TWO{\_}RET{\_}ADDRS}}{PULL:unTWO:unRET:unADDRS}{PYNWmai0-ZQ3HACWSYBTTFFJTEM5GLS64PU000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{PULL{\_}TWO{\_}RET{\_}ADDRS}}{PULL:unTWO:unRET:unADDRS}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1100}\nwdocspar
A 3-byte relay in the handler page.  Jumps to
{\Tt{}RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH\nwendquote} at {\Tt{}{\$}0700\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1101}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-E65AIYYMBOBXUD37K6QZUOOI4I000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-E65AIYYMBOBXUD37K6QZUOOI4I000000-1}}}\moddef{decode dispatch relay~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-E65AIYYMBOBXUD37K6QZUOOI4I000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $07DF
\nwlinkedidentc{DECODE_DISPATCH_RELAY}{PYNWmai0-E65AIYYMBOBXUD37K6QZUOOI4I000000-1}:
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{RESIDENT_DECODE_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-BGQWA2JRONNKYOAYDUITG47DXM000000-1}
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DECODE{\_}DISPATCH{\_}RELAY}}{DECODE:unDISPATCH:unRELAY}{PYNWmai0-E65AIYYMBOBXUD37K6QZUOOI4I000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DECODE{\_}DISPATCH{\_}RELAY}}{DECODE:unDISPATCH:unRELAY}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDECODE:unDISPATCH}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{RESIDENT{\_}DECODE{\_}DISPATCH}}{RESIDENT:unDECODE:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-E65AIYYMBOBXUD37K6QZUOOI4I000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1102}\nwdocspar
The remaining 30 bytes of the handler page ({\Tt{}{\$}07E2--{\$}07FF\nwendquote}) are
unreferenced residual data left over from the EALDR copy
({\Tt{}{\$}A300--{\$}A5FF\nwendquote}~$\to$~{\Tt{}{\$}0500--{\$}07FF\nwendquote}).  Decoded as Apple~II
text the tail reads ``\texttt{SSSSSSSSSS}'' (ten high-bit~S) followed by
the low-ASCII fragment ``\texttt{e checker}''.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1103}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-26YTRDQ4FU3QYCRGXMAVMAHTXA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-26YTRDQ4FU3QYCRGXMAVMAHTXA000000-1}}}\moddef{resident page tail~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-26YTRDQ4FU3QYCRGXMAVMAHTXA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $07E2
; Unused residual data at end of resident handler page
    HEX     0000000e968c41964120
    HEX     d3d3d3d3d3d3d3d3d3d3
    HEX     006520636865636b6572  ; "e checker" (low ASCII)
\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1104}\nwdocspar

\chapter{Disk I/O}

The disk I/O routines occupy {\Tt{}{\$}B300--{\$}BFFF\nwendquote} at runtime.  They are not
loaded directly to their final addresses by the EALDR\@.  Instead, the
EALDR loads them into the hi-res page staging area {\Tt{}{\$}3300--{\$}3FFF\nwendquote}
(tracks~{\Tt{}{\$}0F\nwendquote} and {\Tt{}{\$}10\nwendquote}).  During {\Tt{}GAME{\_}INIT\nwendquote},
{\Tt{}RELOCATE{\_}HIRES{\_}STAGING\nwendquote} copies {\Tt{}{\$}3300--{\$}3FFF\nwendquote} to {\Tt{}{\$}B300--{\$}BFFF\nwendquote}.

These routines handle all disk access for the main game, including
scene loading and save/restore operations.

The area splits into two distinct regions:
\begin{itemize}
\item {\Tt{}{\$}B300--{\$}B5FF\nwendquote}: Custom game disk code---a VM-driven sector
  reader with its own data field verifier, context-swap mechanism,
  and embedded bytecode program.
\item {\Tt{}{\$}B600--{\$}BFFF\nwendquote}: Standard Apple~II DOS~3.3 RWTS (Read/Write
  Track/Sector), byte-identical to the system master after relocation,
  with only three patched bytes:
  \begin{itemize}
  \item {\Tt{}{\$}B748--{\$}B749\nwendquote}: JMP target changed from {\Tt{}{\$}9B03\nwendquote} (DOS
    file manager) to {\Tt{}{\$}9D84\nwendquote} (game's handler).
  \item {\Tt{}{\$}B7E1\nwendquote}: max-drives configuration byte changed from
    {\Tt{}{\$}02\nwendquote} to {\Tt{}{\$}00\nwendquote}.
  \end{itemize}
  The standard RWTS is fully documented in \emph{Beneath Apple DOS}
  (Worth \& Lechner, 1984) and will not be re-disassembled here.
\end{itemize}

The main program calls two entry points in this area:

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{lll}
\toprule
\thead{Address} & \thead{Label} & \thead{Called from} \\
\midrule
{\Tt{}{\$}B300\nwendquote} & {\Tt{}DISK{\_}READ{\_}FIELD\nwendquote} & {\Tt{}LOAD{\_}SCENE{\_}DATA\nwendquote} (\$6DD8) \\
{\Tt{}{\$}B7B5\nwendquote} & {\Tt{}RWTS{\_}ENTRY\nwendquote} & {\Tt{}SETUP{\_}DISK{\_}READ\nwendquote} (\$6C18) \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

The RWTS (Read/Write Track/Sector) at~{\Tt{}{\$}B600--{\$}BFFF\nwendquote} is a copy of
the standard Apple~II DOS~3.3 RWTS from the system master, with the
standard~{\Tt{}{\$}80\nwendquote} page relocation applied (disk pages {\Tt{}{\$}36--{\$}3F\nwendquote}
relocated to~{\Tt{}{\$}B6--{\$}BF\nwendquote}).  Byte-for-byte comparison with the DOS~3.3
System Master (680-0051-00) confirms that all code is identical after
relocation, except for the three patched bytes noted above.  The
remaining differences are runtime state in the IOB parameter block
at~{\Tt{}{\$}B7E8\nwendquote} and the contents of the nibble buffers
at~{\Tt{}{\$}BB00--{\$}BC55\nwendquote}.

The RWTS is fully documented in \emph{Beneath Apple DOS} (Worth \&
Lechner, 1984), chapter~4, and is not disassembled here.  The binary
data is included verbatim from {\Tt{}dos.asm\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1105}{\Tt{}RWTS{\_}CORE\nwendquote} at {\Tt{}{\$}BD00\nwendquote} is the main dispatcher inside the standard
DOS 3.3 RWTS.  {\Tt{}RWTS{\_}ENTRY\nwendquote} calls it after saving the processor status
and disabling interrupts.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1106}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-72}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-72}}}\moddef{defines~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-1}}}\plusendmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwprevnextdefs{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-71}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}\nwenddeflinemarkup
\nwlinkedidentc{RWTS_CORE}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-72}               EQU     $BD00   ; RWTS core dispatcher
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{RWTS{\_}CORE}}{RWTS:unCORE}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-72}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{RWTS{\_}CORE}}{RWTS:unCORE}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1107}\nwdocspar
\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1108}The chunk below includes the standard RWTS binary verbatim, accessed through {\Tt{}RWTS{\_}ENTRY\nwendquote} and {\Tt{}RWTS{\_}CORE\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1109}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-4VZT35TKJMQVI6ZN27HVHBNXQI000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-4VZT35TKJMQVI6ZN27HVHBNXQI000000-1}}}\moddef{dos33 rwts~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-4VZT35TKJMQVI6ZN27HVHBNXQI000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B600
    INCLUDE "dos.asm"
\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1110}\nwdocspar
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}READ{\_}FIELD\nwendquote} is the entry point called by {\Tt{}LOAD{\_}SCENE{\_}DATA\nwendquote}
to read sector data from disk.  It is a trampoline into the disk~VM
interpreter loop at~{\Tt{}{\$}B36A\nwendquote}, which uses an embedded bytecode program
to control the multi-sector read process.  The carry flag is clear on
entry (set by the caller) to indicate a read operation.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1111}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}}}\moddef{disk read field~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B300
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_READ_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}:
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1} ; enter VM interpreter
    NOP                  ; pad
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}READ{\_}FIELD}}{DISK:unREAD:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}READ{\_}FIELD}}{DISK:unREAD:unFIELD}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOOP}}{DISK:unVM:unLOOP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOOP}}{DISK:unVM:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-7Q6U2HMH7OODAMFYNQVO4GCSAA000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1112}\nwdocspar
Searches for the standard data field prologue ({\Tt{}D5\ AA\ AD\nwendquote}) on
the disk, then reads all 342 nibbles (86~secondary + 256~primary) and
verifies each byte equals~{\Tt{}{\$}AF\nwendquote}.  Finally checks the data epilogue
({\Tt{}DE\ AA\nwendquote}).  Returns carry clear on success, carry set on error.

{\Tt{}VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD\nwendquote} is called internally by the disk~VM (via {\Tt{}JSR\ {\$}B304\nwendquote}).

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1113}{\Tt{}DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG\nwendquote} at {\Tt{}{\$}C0EC\nwendquote} is the Disk~II controller's data
register for the active slot.  {\Tt{}VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD\nwendquote} reads nibble bytes
from this register in a tight loop while searching for the D5~AA~AD
prologue and verifying the data field contents.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1114}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}}}\moddef{defines~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-1}}}\plusendmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwprevnextdefs{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-72}{\relax}\nwenddeflinemarkup
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}           EQU     $C0EC   ; disk controller data register (active slot)
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG}}{DISK:unDATA:unREG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG}}{DISK:unDATA:unREG}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1115}\nwdocspar

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1116}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}}}\moddef{verify data field~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B304
\nwlinkedidentc{VERIFY_DATA_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{VERIFY_DATA_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}

; --- Search for D5 AA AD data field prologue ---
    LDY     #$20             ; timeout counter = 32
.search_d5:
    DEY
    BEQ     .error           ; timeout
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read disk nibble
    BPL     *-3              ; wait for data ready
.check_d5:
    EOR     #$D5             ; check for D5
    BNE     .search_d5       ; not D5, retry
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read next nibble
    BPL     *-3
    BANE    #$AA,.check_d5   ; not AA, re-check as D5
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read third nibble
    BPL     *-3
    BANE    #$AD,.check_d5   ; not AD, re-check as D5

; --- Found D5 AA AD: read and verify data nibbles ---
    PHA                      ; \\ waste cycles (timing)
    PLA                      ; /
    LDY     #$56             ; 86 nibbles (secondary buffer)
.read_secondary:
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read nibble
    BPL     *-3
    BIT     $C000            ; timing sync
    BANE    #$AF,.bad_nibble ; mismatch -> error
    DEY
    BNE     .read_secondary

    LDY     #$00             ; 256 nibbles (primary buffer)
.read_primary:
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read nibble
    BPL     *-3
    BIT     $C000            ; timing sync
    BANE    #$AF,.bad_nibble ; mismatch -> error
    DEY
    BNE     .read_primary

; --- Verify DE AA data field epilogue ---
    LDY     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read (discard) checksum nibble
    BPL     *-3
    PHA                      ; \\ waste cycles
    PLA                      ; /
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read epilogue byte 1
    BPL     *-3
    BANE    #$DE,.error      ; bad epilogue
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}    ; read epilogue byte 2
    BPL     *-3
    BAEQ    #$AA,.ok         ; good epilogue

.error:
    SEC                      ; carry set = error
    RTS

.ok:
    CLC                      ; carry clear = success
    RTS

.bad_nibble:
    STA     $B5F6            ; save mismatched nibble for diagnostics
    SEC                      ; carry set = error
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD}}{VERIFY:unDATA:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD}}{VERIFY:unDATA:unFIELD}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG}}{DISK:unDATA:unREG}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG}}{DISK:unDATA:unREG}{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1117}\nwdocspar
The disk~VM interpreter loop.  Each iteration swaps the VM context
(zero-page {\Tt{}{\$}50--{\$}5F\nwendquote} $\leftrightarrow$ saved state at {\Tt{}{\$}B5E5\nwendquote})
and then dispatches the next VM opcode.  The VM bytecode program
follows immediately after the {\Tt{}JMP\nwendquote} at~{\Tt{}{\$}B370\nwendquote} and controls the
multi-sector disk read sequence.  The loop has no explicit exit; the
VM program returns to the caller by manipulating the stack.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1118}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm loop~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B36A
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}     ; swap ZP $50-$5F with saved state
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1} ; execute next VM opcode
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}     ; repeat
; VM bytecode program follows at $B373
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOOP}}{DISK:unVM:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOOP}}{DISK:unVM:unLOOP}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH}}{DISK:unVM:unDISPATCH}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH}}{DISK:unVM:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-YZGRORWYCIPHUUXFRQEDWRNPDM000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1119}\nwdocspar
The VM bytecode program follows immediately after the VM loop.  All
address operands are encrypted (low byte XOR~{\Tt{}{\$}9C\nwendquote}, high byte
XOR~{\Tt{}{\$}AD\nwendquote}); immediate operands are XOR'd with~{\Tt{}{\$}7A\nwendquote}.  The program
implements a disk copy-protection verifier: it checks code integrity,
turns on the drive motor, reads data from multiple half-track positions,
and exits with carry indicating success or failure.

Decoded program flow:

\begin{enumerate}
\item Three integrity checks: verifies bytes at {\Tt{}{\$}B361\nwendquote}, {\Tt{}{\$}B362\nwendquote},
  and {\Tt{}{\$}B368\nwendquote} (the {\Tt{}SEC\nwendquote}, {\Tt{}RTS\nwendquote}, and {\Tt{}SEC\nwendquote} opcodes in
  {\Tt{}VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD\nwendquote}).  Exits with error if any byte was patched.
\item Calls the disk-read subroutine at {\Tt{}{\$}B3A8\nwendquote} (embedded below).
  On failure, retries once; on second failure, allows up to~2 errors
  before restarting.
\item The subroutine turns on the motor, waits for spin-up, sets read
  mode, calls the half-track verifier ({\Tt{}READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY\nwendquote}) from
  multiple head positions, then turns off the motor.
\item On success, exits via {\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK\nwendquote} (carry clear).
\end{enumerate}

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1120}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm bytecode~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B373
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_BYTECODE}{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}:
; --- Integrity checks ---
; LDA_ABS $B361; SUB_IMM #$38; BNZ $B48D  (verify SEC at .error)
    HEX     04 fd 1e 07 42 02 11 19
; LDA_ABS $B362; SUB_IMM #$60; BNZ $B48D  (verify RTS at .error+1)
    HEX     04 fe 1e 07 1a 02 11 19
; LDA_ABS $B368; SUB_IMM #$38; BNZ $B48D  (verify SEC at .bad_nibble+3)
    HEX     04 f4 1e 07 42 02 11 19
; --- First attempt ---
; CALL $B3A8 (disk read sub); BNZ $B394 (retry on error)
    HEX     05 34 1e
; GOTO $B3A5 (exit OK path, skipping retry)
    HEX     02 08 1e ;BNZ $B394 (if error -> retry)
    HEX     0c 39 1e ;GOTO $B3A5 (success -> exit)
; --- Retry (second attempt, at $B394) ---
; CALL $B3A8; BNZ $B39D (error handler)
    HEX     05 34 1e
    HEX     02 01 1e ;BNZ $B39D (if error -> handler)
; GOTO $B3A5 (exit OK)
    HEX     0c 39 1e
; --- Error handler at $B39D ---
; SUB_IMM #$02; BNZ $B48D (fatal if >2 errors); GOTO $B373 (restart)
    HEX     07 78 02 11 19 0c ef 1e
; --- Success exit at $B3A5 ---
; CALL0 $B492 (\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_EXIT_OK}{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1})
    HEX     09 0e 19
; --- Disk read subroutine at $B3A8 ---
; LDA_ABS $C0E9 (motor on)
    HEX     04 75 6d
; LDA_IMM #$FF; CALL1 $FCA8 (ROM WAIT)  x2  (motor spin-up delay)
    HEX     03 85 01 34 51
    HEX     03 85 01 34 51
; LDA_ABS $C0EE (Q7L = read mode)
    HEX     04 72 6d
; LDA_IMM #$00; STA_ABS $B5E2 (clear error accumulator)
    HEX     03 7a 06 7e 18
; CALL $B3D0 x4 (half-track verification passes)
    HEX     05 4c 1e
    HEX     05 4c 1e
    HEX     05 4c 1e
    HEX     05 4c 1e
; LDA_ABS $C0E8 (motor off)
    HEX     04 74 6d
; LDA_ABS $B5E2 (load error count); RET
    HEX     04 7e 18 08
; --- Half-track verifier subroutine at $B3D0 ---
; LDA_IMM #$03; STA_ABS $B44D (set retry counter)
    HEX     03 79 06 d1 19
; CALL1 $B44E (\nwlinkedidentc{READ_AND_VERIFY}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1})
    HEX     01 d2 19
; LDA_ABS $C0E7 (phase 3 on -- half-track step)
    HEX     04 7b 6d
; CALL1 $B44E x2
    HEX     01 d2 19 01 d2 19
; LDA_ABS $C0E1 (phase 0 on -- half-track step)
    HEX     04 7d 6d
; CALL $B46B (PARK_STEPPER sub)
    HEX     05 f7 19
; LDA_IMM #$03; STA_ABS $B44D (reset retry counter)
    HEX     03 79 06 d1 19
; CALL1 $B44E (\nwlinkedidentc{READ_AND_VERIFY}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1})
    HEX     01 d2 19
; LDA_ABS $C0E7 (phase 3 on)
    HEX     04 7b 6d
; CALL1 $B44E x2
    HEX     01 d2 19 01 d2 19
; LDA_ABS $C0E5 (phase 2 on -- half-track step)
    HEX     04 79 6d
; CALL $B46B (PARK_STEPPER); RET
    HEX     05 f7 19 08
; $B3FF: next byte ($A0) is the LDY opcode of \nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_ADDR_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}BYTECODE}}{DISK:unVM:unBYTECODE}{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}BYTECODE}}{DISK:unVM:unBYTECODE}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unOK}}\\{{\nwixident{READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY}}{READ:unAND:unVERIFY}}\\{{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unOK}{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY}}{READ:unAND:unVERIFY}{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-XUCNFCEPISMBQ62XBBUYF4U6FA000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1121}\nwdocspar
Searches for the D5~AA~96 address field prologue on disk.  The entry
point at {\Tt{}{\$}B3FF\nwendquote} sets~Y to~{\Tt{}{\$}FF\nwendquote} (256~inner retries), then falls
through to the search loop.  The outer retry count is loaded from and
saved back to the data byte at~{\Tt{}{\$}B44D\nwendquote}.  On success, returns with
carry clear, X~=~1, and the last decoded address-field nibble
(sector number) in~{\Tt{}{\$}50\nwendquote}.  On failure, returns with carry set.

The 4-and-4 decoding loop reads three field pairs
(volume, track, sector), each encoded as two disk nibbles whose
even/odd bits must be combined via {\Tt{}ROL\nwendquote}/{\Tt{}AND\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1122}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}}}\moddef{search addr field~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B3FF
\nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_ADDR_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}:
    LDY     #$FF               ; 256 inner retries
; $B400: FF is the immediate operand (part of the LDY instruction)
.search:
    LDX     \nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_RETRY_COUNT}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1} ; outer retry count
.next_nibble:
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}      ; read nibble
    BPL     *-3                ; wait for ready
    BAEQ    #$D5,.got_d5       ; first prologue byte?
    DEY                        ; inner countdown
    BNE     .next_nibble
    DEX                        ; outer countdown
    BNE     .next_nibble
    RTS                        ; timeout (carry unchanged)

.got_d5:
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}
    BPL     *-3
    BAEQ    #$AA,.got_aa       ; second prologue byte?
    DEY
    BNE     .next_nibble
    SEC
    RTS                        ; timeout

.got_aa:
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}
    BPL     *-3
    BAEQ    #$96,.got_96       ; third prologue byte (addr mark)?
    DEY
    BNE     .next_nibble
    SEC
    RTS                        ; timeout

.got_96:
; --- Decode 3 address field pairs (4-and-4 encoding) ---
    LDY     #$02               ; 3 pairs: volume, track, sector
.decode_pair:
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}      ; read odd-bits nibble
    BPL     *-3
    ROL                        ; shift data, odd bits in even positions
    STA     $50                ; save partial
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_DATA_REG}{PYNWmai0-4XHAE6ADBEGDO3TDMJXX7ROHNM000000-73}      ; read even-bits nibble
    BPL     *-3
    AND     $50                ; combine: result = decoded byte
    STA     $50                ; last decoded value (sector on final iter)
    DEY
    STX     \nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_RETRY_COUNT}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1} ; save remaining retry count
    BPL     .decode_pair       ; Y: 2->1->0->FF (3 iterations)

    CLC                        ; success
    LDX     #$01
    RTS

\nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_RETRY_COUNT}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}:
    DC.B    3                  ; $B44D: outer retry counter (default 3)
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}RETRY{\_}COUNT}}{SEARCH:unRETRY:unCOUNT}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}}\\{{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}RETRY{\_}COUNT}}{SEARCH:unRETRY:unCOUNT}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG}}{DISK:unDATA:unREG}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}DATA{\_}REG}}{DISK:unDATA:unREG}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1123}\nwdocspar
Native routine called by the VM via CALL1.  Searches for an address
field ({\Tt{}JSR\ {\$}B3FF\nwendquote}), then verifies the data field
({\Tt{}JSR\ VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD\nwendquote}).  Accumulates errors in
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}ERROR{\_}ACCUM\nwendquote} at~{\Tt{}{\$}B5E2\nwendquote}.  Afterward, turns off all
four stepper motor phases (preventing motor heating).

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1124}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}}}\moddef{read and verify~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B44E
\nwlinkedidentc{READ_AND_VERIFY}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{SEARCH_ADDR_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-J2SFLYLBSPJHVFZHPEHN3SZGUE000000-1}   ; find D5 AA 96 address prologue
    BCS     .skip_verify        ; no address field -> skip data verify
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{VERIFY_DATA_FIELD}{PYNWmai0-F7XUTD7KQKIZ2LAWT3LZSLO5AM000000-1}   ; verify D5 AA AD data field
.skip_verify:
    LDA     #$00
    ADC     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_ERROR_ACCUM}{PYNWmai0-3LNGT77F4DQTH3TJC5JDNHUVFY000000-1} ; carry -> error count
    STA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_ERROR_ACCUM}{PYNWmai0-3LNGT77F4DQTH3TJC5JDNHUVFY000000-1}
; --- Turn off all stepper motor phases ---
    LDA     $C0E0               ; phase 0 off
    LDA     $C0E2               ; phase 1 off
    LDA     $C0E4               ; phase 2 off
    LDA     $C0E6               ; phase 3 off
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY}}{READ:unAND:unVERIFY}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{READ{\_}AND{\_}VERIFY}}{READ:unAND:unVERIFY}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}ERROR{\_}ACCUM}}{DISK:unVM:unERROR:unACCUM}}\\{{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}}\\{{\nwixident{VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD}}{VERIFY:unDATA:unFIELD}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}ERROR{\_}ACCUM}}{DISK:unVM:unERROR:unACCUM}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{SEARCH{\_}ADDR{\_}FIELD}}{SEARCH:unADDR:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{VERIFY{\_}DATA{\_}FIELD}}{VERIFY:unDATA:unFIELD}{PYNWmai0-R67OK4YBMROAH3RWLL533FQFDE000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1125}\nwdocspar
VM bytecode subroutine that delays and turns off all stepper motor
phases.  Called via VM opcode~5 (CALL) from the half-track verifier.
The delays allow the stepper motor to settle between half-track
movements.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1126}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-CZ7WUYZMRQOZ776KSWPCNVZLQI000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-CZ7WUYZMRQOZ776KSWPCNVZLQI000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm sub park stepper~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-CZ7WUYZMRQOZ776KSWPCNVZLQI000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B46B
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SUB_PARK_STEPPER}{PYNWmai0-CZ7WUYZMRQOZ776KSWPCNVZLQI000000-1}:
; LDA_IMM #$70; CALL1 $B482 (\nwlinkedidentc{DELAY_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1})  -- long delay
    HEX     03 0a 01 1e 19
; LDA_ABS $C0E0 (phase 0 off)
    HEX     04 7c 6d
; LDA_ABS $C0E2 (phase 1 off)
    HEX     04 7e 6d
; LDA_ABS $C0E4 (phase 2 off)
    HEX     04 78 6d
; LDA_ABS $C0E6 (phase 3 off)
    HEX     04 7a 6d
; LDA_IMM #$28; CALL1 $B482 (\nwlinkedidentc{DELAY_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1})  -- short delay
    HEX     03 52 01 1e 19
; RET
    HEX     08
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SUB{\_}PARK{\_}STEPPER}}{DISK:unVM:unSUB:unPARK:unSTEPPER}{PYNWmai0-CZ7WUYZMRQOZ776KSWPCNVZLQI000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SUB{\_}PARK{\_}STEPPER}}{DISK:unVM:unSUB:unPARK:unSTEPPER}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DELAY{\_}LOOP}}{DELAY:unLOOP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DELAY{\_}LOOP}}{DELAY:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-CZ7WUYZMRQOZ776KSWPCNVZLQI000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1127}\nwdocspar
Native delay routine.  Takes the delay count in~{\Tt{}A\nwendquote} (passed from
VM accumulator via CALL1).  Inner loop: {\Tt{}DEX\nwendquote} from {\Tt{}{\$}20\nwendquote} to~0
(32~iterations).  Outer loop: {\Tt{}SBC{\textasciitilde}{\textbackslash}{\#}{\$}01\nwendquote} until A~reaches~0.
Total delay $\approx$ 160~×~A cycles.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1128}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}}}\moddef{delay loop~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B482
\nwlinkedidentc{DELAY_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DELAY_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}
    LDX     #$20
.inner:
    DEX
    BNE     .inner
    SEC
    SBC     #$01
    BNE     \nwlinkedidentc{DELAY_LOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DELAY{\_}LOOP}}{DELAY:unLOOP}{PYNWmai0-AYPWB6RBUT4LN5CUQ4A26AMLNQ000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DELAY{\_}LOOP}}{DELAY:unLOOP}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1129}\nwdocspar
Short VM bytecode fragment that loads {\Tt{}{\$}20\nwendquote} into the accumulator
and jumps to {\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR\nwendquote}, which swaps context and returns
to the caller with carry set.  Reached by the integrity-check and
error-handler {\Tt{}BNZ\nwendquote} branches in the main bytecode program.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1130}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-ZXIAUF3LVQ4RS7ZIP64X3QRMVM000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-ZXIAUF3LVQ4RS7ZIP64X3QRMVM000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm sub exit err~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-ZXIAUF3LVQ4RS7ZIP64X3QRMVM000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B48D
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SUB_EXIT_ERR}{PYNWmai0-ZXIAUF3LVQ4RS7ZIP64X3QRMVM000000-1}:
; LDA_IMM #$20; CALL0 $B497 (\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_EXIT_ERR}{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1})
    HEX     03 5a 09 0b 19
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SUB{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{DISK:unVM:unSUB:unEXIT:unERR}{PYNWmai0-ZXIAUF3LVQ4RS7ZIP64X3QRMVM000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SUB{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{DISK:unVM:unSUB:unEXIT:unERR}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unERR}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unERR}{PYNWmai0-ZXIAUF3LVQ4RS7ZIP64X3QRMVM000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1131}\nwdocspar
These native routines exit the disk~VM\@.  Each calls
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP\nwendquote} to restore the caller's zero-page state, then
returns with carry flag indicating success or failure.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1132}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm exit ok~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B492
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_EXIT_OK}{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1} ; restore caller's ZP
    CLC                  ; carry clear = success
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unOK}{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}OK}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unOK}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-GPMJOZWLZ5RVOPUPG24SCBP6HY000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1133}\nwdocspar
\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1134}{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR\nwendquote} is the error counterpart: it swaps context back and
returns with carry set, signalling failure to the caller.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1135}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm exit err~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B497
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_EXIT_ERR}{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1} ; restore caller's ZP
    SEC                  ; carry set = error
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unERR}{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}EXIT{\_}ERR}}{DISK:unVM:unEXIT:unERR}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-3PDBUTNAY2LH74GXKBLMC3S7IE000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1136}\nwdocspar
\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1137}{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}MISC{\_}B49C\nwendquote} is a two-byte helper that XORs~A with~\$AD and
returns.  It is used by the VM opcode decoder to decrypt the high byte of
address operands (the encryption key for high bytes is~\$AD).

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1138}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-BEPMJ3SFV2UOCBYC5OUXROTXJA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-BEPMJ3SFV2UOCBYC5OUXROTXJA000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm misc b49c~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-BEPMJ3SFV2UOCBYC5OUXROTXJA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B49C
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_MISC_B49C}{PYNWmai0-BEPMJ3SFV2UOCBYC5OUXROTXJA000000-1}:
    EOR     #$AD
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}MISC{\_}B49C}}{DISK:unVM:unMISC:unB49C}{PYNWmai0-BEPMJ3SFV2UOCBYC5OUXROTXJA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}MISC{\_}B49C}}{DISK:unVM:unMISC:unB49C}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1139}\nwdocspar
Swaps 16~bytes between zero-page locations {\Tt{}{\$}50--{\$}5F\nwendquote} and the
saved VM state at {\Tt{}{\$}B5E5--{\$}B5F4\nwendquote}.  This preserves the caller's
zero-page state while the VM executes, and restores it afterward.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1140}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm swap~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B4CC
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SWAP}{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}

    LDX     #$0F    ; 16 bytes ($50-$5F)
.loop:
    LDA     $50,X   ; \\ swap ZP with saved state
    PHA             ;  |
    LDA     $B5E5,X ;  |
    STA     $50,X   ;  |
    PLA             ;  |
    STA     $B5E5,X ; /
    DEX
    BPL     .loop
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}{PYNWmai0-YFYCU6Q2EWD6JQJOO76Z4EFCFU000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP}}{DISK:unVM:unSWAP}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1141}\nwdocspar
Scratch/data area between {\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}SWAP\nwendquote} and the opcode handlers.
Contains mostly zeros with bytes~{\Tt{}{\$}4A\ {\$}4C\ {\$}4E\nwendquote} at~{\Tt{}{\$}B4EE--{\$}B4F0\nwendquote},
mirroring the initial saved-state values for ZP~{\Tt{}{\$}5B--{\$}5D\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1142}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-XH5TLQEXYFHHOOER46HK6GDTMY000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-XH5TLQEXYFHHOOER46HK6GDTMY000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm data b4de~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-XH5TLQEXYFHHOOER46HK6GDTMY000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B4DE
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_DATA_B4DE}{PYNWmai0-XH5TLQEXYFHHOOER46HK6GDTMY000000-1}:
    HEX     00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
    HEX     4a 4c 4e
    HEX     00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00 00
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DATA{\_}B4DE}}{DISK:unVM:unDATA:unB4DE}{PYNWmai0-XH5TLQEXYFHHOOER46HK6GDTMY000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DATA{\_}B4DE}}{DISK:unVM:unDATA:unB4DE}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1143}\nwdocspar
Executes one VM opcode.  Pulls the return address from the stack to
locate the current instruction pointer, reads the next opcode byte,
looks up the handler address (low byte) in the table at~{\Tt{}{\$}B4BF\nwendquote},
and self-modifies a {\Tt{}JMP\nwendquote} at~{\Tt{}{\$}B5DF\nwendquote} to dispatch to the
handler in page~{\Tt{}{\$}B5\nwendquote}.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1144}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm dispatch~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B49F
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}

    TXA
    PHA                           ; save X
    TYA
    PHA                           ; save Y
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_PULL_IP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}       ; pull IP from stack -> $52/$53
    PLA
    STA     $52                   ; \\ restore as VM instruction pointer
    PLA                           ;  | (values set by \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_PULL_IP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}
    STA     $53                   ; /  via stack manipulation)
    LDY     #$04
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_RESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}:
    LDA     ($52),Y               ; read opcode from VM program
    INY
    BNE     .no_page
    INC     $53
.no_page:
    TAX
    LDA     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_HANDLER_TBL}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1},X ; look up handler low byte
    STA     $B5E0                 ; self-modify JMP target at $B5DF
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_TRAMPOLINE}{PYNWmai0-52EDTY2P6XACMBHKFKS45MCWBU000000-1}    ; dispatch (JMP $B5xx)

; --- VM opcode handler table ---
; Each byte is the low address of a handler in page $B5.
; Opcode index -> handler address ($B5xx).
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_HANDLER_TBL}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}:
    HEX     79 27 55 5f 6d 8a ad b9 a2 4f ce 26 00
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH}}{DISK:unVM:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}HANDLER{\_}TBL}}{DISK:unVM:unHANDLER:unTBL}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH}}{DISK:unVM:unDISPATCH}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}HANDLER{\_}TBL}}{DISK:unVM:unHANDLER:unTBL}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}PULL{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unPULL:unIP}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}TRAMPOLINE}}{DISK:unVM:unTRAMPOLINE}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}PULL{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unPULL:unIP}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}TRAMPOLINE}}{DISK:unVM:unTRAMPOLINE}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1145}\nwdocspar
\nwenddocs{}\nwbegindocs{1146}{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}PULL{\_}IP\nwendquote} is an RTS-trick bootstrap used during VM startup.  It
pulls two return addresses from the 6502 stack: the inner one (from the JSR
that called it) and the outer one (from the JSR that entered
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH\nwendquote}).  The outer address becomes the VM instruction pointer
({\Tt{}{\$}52/{\$}53\nwendquote}), and both are incremented by~1 to compensate for the 6502's
convention of pushing address$-$1 during JSR.  Execution then resumes at the
inner return address via an indirect jump, allowing the dispatcher to
``capture'' its caller's position as the starting bytecode address.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1147}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm pull ip~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B538
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_PULL_IP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_PULL_IP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}

; Pulls two return addresses from the stack.
; The outer return address (from JSR \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}) becomes the
; VM instruction pointer.  Resumes execution at the inner return
; address + 1 (i.e., returns to the code after JSR \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_PULL_IP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}).
    PLA               ; \\ inner return address
    STA     $54       ;  | (from JSR \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_PULL_IP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1})
    PLA               ; /
    STA     $55
    PLA               ; \\ outer return address
    STA     $52       ;  | (from JSR \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_DISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1})
    PLA               ; /  = VM instruction pointer
    STA     $53
    INC     $52       ; adjust IP (6502 pushes addr-1)
    INC     $54       ; \\ adjust inner return address
    BNE     .no_carry ;  |
    INC     $55       ; /
.no_carry:
    JMP     ($54)     ; resume at inner return + 1
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}PULL{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unPULL:unIP}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}PULL{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unPULL:unIP}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH}}{DISK:unVM:unDISPATCH}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH}}{DISK:unVM:unDISPATCH}{PYNWmai0-A5B4HCW5L3AKHGBTUDGDGGKD7U000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1148}\nwdocspar
Shared subroutine used by most VM opcode handlers.  Reads two
encrypted bytes from the bytecode stream at~{\Tt{}{\$}52/{\$}53\ +\ Y\nwendquote}, decrypts
the address operand (low byte XOR~{\Tt{}{\$}9C\nwendquote}, high byte XOR~{\Tt{}{\$}AD\nwendquote}),
stores the result in~{\Tt{}{\$}54/{\$}55\nwendquote}, and advances~Y past both bytes.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1149}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm read addr~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B510
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1}:
    LDA     ($52),Y ; read encrypted low byte
    EOR     #$9C    ; decrypt
    INY
    BNE     .no_page1
    INC     $53
.no_page1:
    STA     $54     ; operand low
    LDA     ($52),Y ; read encrypted high byte
    INY
    BNE     .no_page2
    INC     $53
.no_page2:
    EOR     #$AD    ; decrypt
    STA     $55     ; operand high
    RTS
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1150}\nwdocspar
The disk~VM has 13~opcodes (indices 0--12).  Each handler's low
address is stored in the table at~{\Tt{}{\$}B4BF\nwendquote}; dispatch jumps to
{\Tt{}{\$}B5xx\nwendquote}.  Handlers return to the dispatch loop by jumping to
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME\nwendquote} at~{\Tt{}{\$}B4AE\nwendquote}, which reads the next opcode and
dispatches again.

\begin{center}
\begin{tabular}{rllp{5cm}}
\toprule
\thead{Op} & \thead{Addr} & \thead{Mnemonic} & \thead{Description} \\
\midrule
 0 & \$B579 & LDA\_IDX  & ACC $\leftarrow$ *(addr + ACC) \\
 1 & \$B527 & CALL1     & Call native; A=ACC in, A$\to$ACC out \\
 2 & \$B555 & BNZ       & Branch if ACC $\ne$ 0 \\
 3 & \$B55F & LDA\_IMM  & ACC $\leftarrow$ immediate (XOR \$7A) \\
 4 & \$B56D & LDA\_ABS  & ACC $\leftarrow$ *(addr) \\
 5 & \$B58A & CALL      & VM subroutine call (push IP) \\
 6 & \$B5AD & STA\_ABS  & *(addr) $\leftarrow$ ACC \\
 7 & \$B5B9 & SUB\_IMM  & ACC $\leftarrow$ ACC $-$ immediate \\
 8 & \$B5A2 & RET       & VM subroutine return (pop IP) \\
 9 & \$B54F & CALL0     & Jump to native code (no return) \\
10 & \$B5CE & INC\_LOAD & *(addr) += 1; ACC $\leftarrow$ result \\
11 & \$B526 & HALT      & Exit VM (RTS) \\
12 & \$B500 & GOTO      & Unconditional branch \\
\bottomrule
\end{tabular}
\end{center}

Unconditional branch.  Reads a 2-byte encrypted address operand and
sets the VM instruction pointer to that address.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1151}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op goto~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B500
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_GOTO}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1} ; read target -> $54/$55
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SET_IP}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}:
    LDA     $54               ; \\ set IP = operand
    STA     $52               ;  |
    LDA     $55               ;  |
    STA     $53               ; /
    LDY     #$00
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_RESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}    ; dispatch next opcode
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}GOTO}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unGOTO}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SET{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unSET:unIP}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}GOTO}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unGOTO}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SET{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unSET:unIP}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1152}\nwdocspar
Exits the VM by executing {\Tt{}RTS\nwendquote}, which returns to the caller of
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}DISPATCH\nwendquote}.  This handler is just the {\Tt{}RTS\nwendquote} at the end
of {\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR\nwendquote}---the dispatch table points to~{\Tt{}{\$}B526\nwendquote}
which is that instruction.

(No separate chunk needed; {\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR\nwendquote} provides the {\Tt{}RTS\nwendquote}.)

Calls a native 6502 subroutine.  Passes the VM accumulator in~{\Tt{}A\nwendquote}
and captures the subroutine's return value from~{\Tt{}A\nwendquote} back into the
accumulator.  The~Y register (bytecode offset) is preserved across
the call.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1153}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op call1~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B527
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_CALL1}{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_CALL1}{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1} ; read native addr -> $54/$55
    TYA
    PHA                       ; save Y (bytecode offset)
    LDA     $56               ; A = VM accumulator
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_JMP_IND}{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}   ; call native routine via JMP ($54)
    STA     $56               ; capture return A -> accumulator
    PLA
    TAY                       ; restore Y
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_RESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}    ; dispatch next opcode
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}CALL1}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unCALL1}{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}CALL1}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unCALL1}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}JMP{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unJMP:unIND}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}JMP{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unJMP:unIND}{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}{PYNWmai0-FHYJF4PP462QWUAWT2MYOPQZQA000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1154}\nwdocspar
Jumps to a native 6502 routine and does not return to the VM\@.
Used for operations like ``enter the main program'' or ``jump to
a disk routine'' that take over control flow.  The {\Tt{}JMP\ ({\$}54)\nwendquote}
at~{\Tt{}{\$}B552\nwendquote} also serves as a reusable trampoline for opcode~1.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1155}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op call0~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B54F
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_CALL0}{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1} ; read native addr -> $54/$55
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_JMP_IND}{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}:
    JMP     ($54)             ; indirect jump (no return to VM)
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}JMP{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unJMP:unIND}{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}CALL0}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unCALL0}{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}JMP{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unJMP:unIND}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}CALL0}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unCALL0}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-6ROXAR4TXGMCPDTWBEIOUIUDBY000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1156}\nwdocspar
Branch if the VM accumulator is nonzero.  Reads a 2-byte encrypted
target address, then either branches (reuses {\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}SET{\_}IP\nwendquote})
or falls through to the next opcode.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1157}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op bnz~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B555
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_BNZ}{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1} ; read branch target -> $54/$55
    LDA     $56               ; test accumulator
    BNE     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_SET_IP}{PYNWmai0-6V42YT5TYM2DEE5FKAVFCDQSX4000000-1}    ; nonzero -> branch (reuse GOTO tail)
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_RESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}    ; zero -> continue
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}BNZ}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unBNZ}{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}BNZ}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unBNZ}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SET{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unSET:unIP}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}SET{\_}IP}}{DISK:unVM:unSET:unIP}{PYNWmai0-BBUI5IBSOBSYGICTQMG22WDXIU000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1158}\nwdocspar
Loads an immediate value into the VM accumulator.  The operand is a
single encrypted byte (XOR~{\Tt{}{\$}7A\nwendquote}).

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1159}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-YYOHR72I2K632KNEY4FRZXJYXY000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YYOHR72I2K632KNEY4FRZXJYXY000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op lda imm~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-YYOHR72I2K632KNEY4FRZXJYXY000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B55F
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_LDA_IMM}{PYNWmai0-YYOHR72I2K632KNEY4FRZXJYXY000000-1}:
    LDA     ($52),Y ; read encrypted byte
    INY
    BNE     .no_page
    INC     $53
.no_page:
    EOR     #$7A    ; decrypt
    STA     $56     ; accumulator = value
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_RESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}LDA{\_}IMM}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unLDA:unIMM}{PYNWmai0-YYOHR72I2K632KNEY4FRZXJYXY000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}LDA{\_}IMM}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unLDA:unIMM}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}{PYNWmai0-YYOHR72I2K632KNEY4FRZXJYXY000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1160}\nwdocspar
Loads a byte from an absolute address into the VM accumulator.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1161}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op lda abs~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B56D
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_LDA_ABS}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}:
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1} ; read address -> $54/$55
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_LOAD_IND}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}:
    LDX     #$00
    LDA     ($54,X)           ; ACC = *(address)
    STA     $56
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_RESUME}{PYNWmai0-3WUSACKGWRVXW4WEKG7CMO6Q2Y000000-1}
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOAD{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unLOAD:unIND}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}LDA{\_}ABS}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unLDA:unABS}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOAD{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unLOAD:unIND}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}LDA{\_}ABS}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unLDA:unABS}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}RESUME}}{DISK:unVM:unRESUME}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1162}\nwdocspar
Indexed load.  Reads a 2-byte base address, adds the VM accumulator
as an offset, then loads the byte at (base~+~ACC) into the
accumulator.  Useful for table lookups.  Falls into
{\Tt{}DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOAD{\_}IND\nwendquote} to perform the actual load.

\nwenddocs{}\nwbegincode{1163}\sublabel{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}\nwmargintag{{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}}}\moddef{disk vm op lda idx~{\nwtagstyle{}\subpageref{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}}}\endmoddef\nwstartdeflinemarkup\nwusesondefline{\\{PYNWmai0-4RSQDT2TWU2QLEUIDKRLWKU4JE000000-1}}\nwenddeflinemarkup
    ORG     $B579
\nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_LDA_IDX}{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}:
    SUBROUTINE \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_OP_LDA_IDX}{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}
    JSR     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_READ_ADDR}{PYNWmai0-ZISC3PL4VWECRONWMEC26G53VU000000-1} ; read base addr -> $54/$55
    LDA     $56               ; index = old accumulator
    CLC
    ADC     $54               ; base_lo + index
    STA     $54
    BCC     .no_carry
    INC     $55               ; propagate carry
.no_carry:
    JMP     \nwlinkedidentc{DISK_VM_LOAD_IND}{PYNWmai0-NZ7I5IVD6ZTADYVYPTUJZ74JME000000-1}  ; load from ($54/$55) -> ACC
\nwindexdefn{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}LDA{\_}IDX}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unLDA:unIDX}{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}\eatline
\nwidentdefs{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}OP{\_}LDA{\_}IDX}}{DISK:unVM:unOP:unLDA:unIDX}}}\nwidentuses{\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOAD{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unLOAD:unIND}}\\{{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}}}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}LOAD{\_}IND}}{DISK:unVM:unLOAD:unIND}{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}\nwindexuse{\nwixident{DISK{\_}VM{\_}READ{\_}ADDR}}{DISK:unVM:unREAD:unADDR}{PYNWmai0-IZL4O3A7R7NJ65AFOK2WBU3GKA000000-1}\nwendcode{}\nwbegindocs{1164}\nwdocspar
VM-level subroutine call.  Pushes the current absolute IP onto the
6502 hardware stack, then branches to the operand address.
